Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents a single play session of the RemoteDisplayLink application, managing its overall state and real-time display synchronization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the session was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the session was last updated.

status.description: The current operational status of the session (e.g., 'pending', 'active', 'completed', 'paused').

totalIterations.description: The total number of iterations planned for this session, fixed at 125.

activeIterationId.description: Reference to the currently active Iteration within this session. Null if no iteration is currently active. (Relationship: Session 1:1 Iteration)

activeIterationStartTime.description: Timestamp when the timer for the current active iteration started. Null if no iteration is active or timer not started.

lastSelectedOptionDisplay.description: The 'ABCD' option prominently displayed on the display panel as the last selection for the active iteration. Null if no option is being displayed.

displayPrompt.description: Indicates the specific prompt or content the display panel should currently show (e.g., 'timer', 'optionSelected', 'nextQuestionPrompt', 'skipPrompt', 'sessionEnded').

Iteration.title: Iteration
Iteration.description: Represents one of the 125 individual cycles or 'questions' within a RemoteDisplayLink session, tracking its progress and outcome.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Iteration entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the Session this iteration belongs to. (Relationship: Session 1:N Iteration)

iterationNumber.description: The sequential number (1-125) of this iteration within its session.

status.description: The current status of this iteration (e.g., 'pending', 'active', 'answered', 'skipped', 'timedOut').

selectedOption.description: The 'ABCD' option chosen for this iteration. Null if not answered, skipped, or timed out.

selectionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when an option was selected for this iteration. Null if not selected.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when this iteration became active and its timer started. Null if not yet active.

endedAt.description: Timestamp when this iteration concluded (answered, skipped, or timed out). Null if still active or pending.

durationSeconds.description: The duration in seconds from 'startedAt' to 'endedAt' for this iteration. Null if not ended.

definition.description: Represents a single play session between two devices. Includes 'controlPanelUid' (the initiating device), 'displayPanelUid' (the joining device), and a 'sessionCode' for joining, ensuring authorization independence for direct access. All updates and reads are secured via these UIDs.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Session document.

definition.description: Represents an individual cycle or 'question' within a Session. Includes denormalized 'controlPanelUid' and 'displayPanelUid' from its parent Session to ensure authorization independence and allow atomic operations without requiring a 'get()' to the parent.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Identifier of the parent Session this Iteration belongs to.

params.name: iterationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Iteration document within its Session.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for RemoteDisplayLink prioritizes secure, real-time synchronization between two devices (a control panel and a display panel) by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The central entity is the `Session`, which orchestrates the real-time interaction and progress across 125 `Iteration` cycles. The key challenge, 'how to connect two devices', is addressed by introducing a `sessionCode` field in the `Session` document and explicitly tracking the `controlPanelUid` and `displayPanelUid`.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**
To eliminate `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, authorization context is denormalized. Each `Session` document will store the `uid` of the controlling device in `controlPanelUid` and the `uid` of the displaying device in `displayPanelUid` (initially null upon creation). Crucially, every `Iteration` document, residing in a subcollection of a `Session`, will *also* contain these `controlPanelUid` and `displayPanelUid` fields, copied from its parent `Session`. This ensures that security rules for `Iteration` documents can evaluate access solely based on the data within the `Iteration` document itself, without needing to fetch the parent `Session` document.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns):**
1.  **`/sessions/{sessionId}`:** This top-level collection holds all `Session` documents. All documents in this collection share a homogeneous security posture: they are owned by a `controlPanelUid` and can be joined by a `displayPanelUid`. To allow a display panel to 'connect', a unique, human-readable `sessionCode` is added to each `Session` document. A display device can query this collection using the `sessionCode` and `displayPanelUid == null` to find and join a session. Control panel devices can list their own sessions by querying `controlPanelUid == request.auth.uid`. These operations are secure and efficient, supporting QAPs, as the necessary authorization fields (`controlPanelUid`, `displayPanelUid`, `sessionCode`) are directly on the `Session` document and can be indexed.
2.  **`/sessions/{sessionId}/iterations/{iterationId}`:** `Iteration` documents are nested within their respective `Session` documents. The denormalized `controlPanelUid` and `displayPanelUid` fields ensure that `list` operations on this subcollection (e.g., to fetch all iterations for an active session) remain secure and efficient. Security rules can directly verify `request.auth.uid` against these denormalized fields, satisfying QAPs without complex filtering or expensive lookups.

**DBAC & Access Modeling:**
Authorization is database-backed. The `controlPanelUid` and `displayPanelUid` fields in each document define the roles (controller/display) directly within the data. Path-based ownership (`/sessions/{sessionId}/iterations/{iterationId}`) is used for hierarchical data, and a simplified 'membership map' (via dedicated `uid` fields) is employed for collaborative data access between the two devices.

This structure ensures clear, explicit, and debuggable security rules, minimal transactional complexities, and high scalability for real-time synchronization between connected devices.
